name: Aleman, Nancy
evidence: Retired Austin resident, zip 78745. Common name with multiple matches in TX public records; identity could not be confirmed to the specific donor and no verifiable policy-advocacy profile was found.

name: Goleman, Kinnan
resolved_employer: KG Strategies, LLC (founder/president); Of Counsel, Earth & Water Law Group — environmental/energy lobbyist & attorney
evidence: Confirmed as Ronald Kinnan Golemon, longtime Austin environmental/energy lobbyist and attorney; TEC-registered lobbyist at 1212 Guadalupe St #102, Austin 78701 (matches donor zip). Former Partner at Brown McCarroll and General Counsel of the Texas Chemical Council (1992-2006).

affiliations.snippet: Served as General Counsel for the Texas Chemical Council, the association representing ~90 chemical manufacturing operations in Texas.

affiliations.org: Registered Texas lobbyist (clients incl. Shell Oil Co., Devon Energy)
affiliations.snippet: TEC-registered lobbyist; Texas legislative witness lists show him representing Shell Oil Co. and Devon Energy Corp. on energy/environmental matters.

name: McGarrahan, Ed
evidence: Retired East Austin resident. A City of Austin document lists 'Ed and Letty McGarrahan, 1105 East 8th Street, Austin' in the 78702 zip matching the donor record, corroborating location; likely spouse of Letesia Cantu-McGarrahan (McGarrahan Architects). No verifiable policy-advocacy or board profile found.

name: Kakroo, Upasna
resolved_employer: Peerbagh (nonprofit founder/leader); writer/author; prior marketing roles at University of Michigan, McKinsey, Rocket Internet
evidence: Writer/author in Ann Arbor, MI (48103). Founder of Peerbagh, a nonprofit promoting South-Asia-inspired storytelling and a children's magazine (Bento); ~20 yrs in marketing/comms. Cultural/storytelling nonprofit, not policy advocacy.

name: Browder, Joseph
resolved_employer: OHT Partners (Austin multifamily real-estate development/construction firm) — self-reported; specific title unconfirmed
evidence: Donor self-reports employer OHT Partners in real estate, zip 78703 (West Austin). OHT Partners is a confirmed Austin-based Class A multifamily developer/GC (formerly Oden Hughes). The firm's public leadership page lists a 'Ben Browder' (Principal) but no 'Joseph Browder'; Joseph's specific role could not be confirmed.

affiliations.snippet: OHT Partners is a Texas multifamily development, construction and investment company focused on Class A properties in Austin and other TX markets; donor lists it as employer with a real-estate occupation — a real-estate-development business interest.

name: Ford, Jenny
evidence: Self-reported stay-at-home mom in 78702 (East Austin). No public profile could be uniquely tied to this specific person; multiple unrelated 'Jenny/Jennifer Ford' records exist in Texas. Cannot corroborate identity beyond the filing itself.

name: Qadri, Zohaib
evidence: Zohaib 'Zo' Qadri, elected 2022, is the Austin City Council Member for District 9 and the first Muslim elected to the Austin Council. Identity confirmed by occupation/employer on filing matching the sitting D9 member.

affiliations.org: Austin City Council (joint council-member statement, Dec. 2023)
affiliations.snippet: Qadri, with council members Vanessa Fuentes and José Velásquez, issued a joint statement on the Israel-Hamas war calling for 'the immediate release of all hostages and a permanent cease-fire' and pledging to resist 'antisemitism, Islamophobia and all other forms of hate.' No FEC pro-Israel/pro-Palestine PAC contributions or TEC lobbyist registration found.

name: Cantu-McGarrahan, Letesia
evidence: Letesia Cantu-McGarrahan operates d/b/a Cantu-McGarrahan Architects, a small Austin residential architecture firm (Travis County court records, city permits, LinkedIn 'Letty McGarrahan'). Name + firm + Austin + owner/architect occupation all corroborate.

name: Renteria, Pio
resolved_employer: Former Austin City Council Member, District 3 (2015-2023); retired IBM technician
evidence: Sabino 'Pio' Renteria represented Austin District 3 from 2015 until 2023, when José Velásquez succeeded him; lifelong East Austin resident who retired from IBM after 34 years. Identity confirmed by occupation ('Councilman'), zip 78702, and predecessor-to-Velásquez status.

name: Sugg, Paul
resolved_employer: Harris County (Sr. Intergovernmental & Global Affairs Specialist); previously Legislative Director / Governmental Relations, Texas Association of Counties
evidence: Paul Sugg was the longtime Legislative Director in Governmental Relations at the Texas Association of Counties (a county-lobbying association at the Texas Legislature) and per LinkedIn now serves as Senior Intergovernmental & Global Affairs Specialist for Harris County. Corroborated by TAC staff directory, legislative witness lists, and LinkedIn.

affiliations.role: Legislative Director / Governmental Relations (intergovernmental legislative advocacy for Texas counties)
affiliations.snippet: Sugg served in TAC Governmental Relations directing county-interest legislative advocacy at the Texas Capitol and appears repeatedly on legislative committee witness lists; intergovernmental/legislative-affairs work rather than a private-client cause. No pro-Israel/pro-Palestine, oil & gas, or gun PAC ties found in FEC records.

name: Harding, Vincent
resolved_employer: Texas Alliance of Boys and Girls Clubs (Executive Director), Houston, TX; employment/ethics attorney
evidence: Vincent Alexander Harding, employment/ethics attorney and former (youngest-ever) Chair of the Travis County Democratic Party; ran for Austin City Council District 1 in 2018. Now Executive Director of the Texas Alliance of Boys and Girls Clubs, based in Houston (zip 77019), corroborating the donor record's Houston/Boys & Girls Clubs occupation.

affiliations.snippet: Harding was the youngest person and second Black man to serve as Chair of the Travis County Democratic Party; later a 2018 Austin City Council D1 candidate.

name: Deverakonda, Puja
resolved_employer: GLIDE (GLIDE Foundation), San Francisco — Senior Manager, Strategy & Operations (formerly Manager, Impact & Strategy)
evidence: Puja Deverakonda is a Senior Manager, Strategy & Operations at GLIDE, the San Francisco social-justice nonprofit; prior roles at Mental Health Association of SF and Hamilton Families. Zip 94114 is San Francisco, matching the GLIDE/Manager occupation in the donor record.

name: Davila, LF
evidence: Donor listed as retired in Buda, TX (zip 78610). Multiple Davilas live in 78610, but none could be tied to the specific 'LF Davila' initials, retired status, or any policy-advocacy/political record. Identity not confirmable beyond a retired Buda resident.

name: Sookiasian, Richard?
resolved_employer: Retired; formerly a registered Texas lobbyist (registered 2009)
evidence: Donor record (retired, Austin 78745) matches Richard Sookiasian, listed in the 2009 Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registry at 7707 Doncaster Dr., Austin, TX 78745 — the exact 78745 zip in the donor record. TEC ID 00061372, registered 02/05/2009-12/31/2009. Now retired. Specific 2009 clients could not be retrieved, so no issue-based affiliation is asserted.

affiliations.org: Texas Ethics Commission — Registered Lobbyist (clients not identified)
affiliations.snippet: Richard Sookiasian, 7707 Doncaster Dr., Austin, TX 78745, TEC ID 00061372, registered as a Texas lobbyist 02/05/2009-12/31/2009; the represented clients/subject matter were not recoverable from the public list.

name: Ortiz, Jr., Solomon
resolved_employer: Ortiz Holdings LLC (Partner / Director of Governmental Affairs); former Texas State Representative, HD 33/34 (Corpus Christi)
evidence: Solomon P. Ortiz Jr., son of former U.S. Rep. Solomon Ortiz, served in the Texas House (Corpus Christi, ~2007-2011) and now works in governmental affairs at Ortiz Holdings LLC; appears in Texas Ethics Commission registered-lobbyist records. Zip 78414 / Corpus Christi and governmental-affairs occupation match.

affiliations.snippet: Solomon P. Ortiz Jr. served as Texas State Representative for the Corpus Christi-area district (roughly 2007-2011); son of former U.S. Rep. Solomon Ortiz.

affiliations.role: Registered lobbyist / Director of Governmental Affairs, Ortiz Holdings
affiliations.snippet: Solomon P. Ortiz Jr. appears in Texas Ethics Commission registered-lobbyist records (e.g., 2017), consistent with his governmental-affairs role.

name: Maciel?, Rose
resolved_employer: Joe's Bakery & Coffee Shop, East Austin — President / co-owner
evidence: Rose Ann Maciel is president/co-owner of Joe's Bakery & Coffee Shop (family restaurant founded 1962 in East Austin, zip 78702); eldest daughter of founder Joe Avila and mother of general manager Regina Estrada. Zip and 'restaurant owner' occupation confirm.

name: Billingsley, Anne
resolved_employer: ONEOK, Inc. — Director, Government Affairs / Government Relations (Austin); registered Texas lobbyist
evidence: Anne Billingsley is Director of Government Affairs/Government Relations for ONEOK, Inc., a major natural-gas pipeline company. She is a registered lobbyist with the Texas Ethics Commission (Austin) and has testified before the Texas Legislature on ONEOK's behalf. Austin base and public-affairs/ONEOK occupation match.

affiliations.role: Director, Government Affairs / registered Texas lobbyist
affiliations.snippet: Billingsley is Director of Government Relations for ONEOK, a natural-gas pipeline company, and a registered Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist who has testified before the Texas Legislature as ONEOK's government-relations representative.

name: Flores, Lucy
resolved_employer: Luz Collective / Luz Media — Co-Founder (Latina-focused digital media); former VP Public Affairs, mitú; former Nevada State Assemblywoman
evidence: Lucy Flores is a former Nevada State Assemblywoman (District 28, 2010-2014) and media entrepreneur who co-founded Luz Collective/Luz Media, a Latina-focused digital media company, after serving as VP of Public Affairs at Latino media company mitú. Matches the 'Co-Founder/Luz Collective' occupation.

affiliations.snippet: Flores was one of the first Latinas elected to the Nevada Legislature, serving Assembly District 28 from 2010-2014; later a 2014 candidate for NV Lieutenant Governor and 2016 congressional candidate.
